lines.text: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, everyone is there having breakfast and Joey enters carrying a loaf of bread.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who wants French toast?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'll have some!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good, me too. (Tosses him the loaf.) Eggs and milk are in the fridge. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering from her room) Oww!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's the matter honey?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know, my hand feels weird. I guess it's because, I'm engaged! (Shows off the ring.) How long before it starts getting annoying?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It starts?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, so let's get started on the wedding plans!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! (Runs off.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (incredulous) Already?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, we got a lot to do! We gotta think about the flowers, the caterers, the music...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I got some thoughts on that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wait Chandler, too many cooks...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Take from me, as the groom all you have to do is show up and try to say the right name.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (returning) Okay! (Sets down a huge 3" 3-ring binder on the table.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What in God's name is that?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God, the wedding book?! I haven't seen that since the forth grade!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This baby has got everything. Take y'know, locations for instance. (She opens up the binder to the locations chapter.) First, organized alphabetically, then geographically, then by square footage.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is so smart! (To Chandler, under her breath) Break it off. Break it off now.
Opening Credits
A Classroom, Ross is giving a lecture.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And that should conclusively prove that I had the idea for Jurassic Park first! Now let's take a look at... (Phoebe rushes in.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, oh my God! Wh-wh-what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I need to talk to you, it's pretty urgent. It's about Monica and Chandler.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! Of course, of course. (To the class.) Umm, would you please excuse me for a moment? Umm, do you know each other's hometowns? Why don't you... (Motions that they should learn everyone's hometown.) (To Phoebe) Wh-what's going on?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, umm, not much. But, I was just thinking that since those guys just got engaged that maybe it would be nice if they had some privacy, y'know? So, could I just move in with you for a couple days?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, okay, yeah, sure. But wh-what's wrong with Monica and Chandler?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nothing-Why?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, you said it was urgent!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah it is! I'm going to the movies and it starts in like five minutes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you realize I have a classroom full of students?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to the students) Oh, I'm sorry. I'm so rude. Does anyone want to come to the movies?
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Rachel and Monica are pretty much telling Chandler what the wedding plans are.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, so I haven't cleared the budget with my parents yet, but tell me how this is for music.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right umm, a string quartet for the procession.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aw.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: A jazz trio for cocktails. The Bay City Rollers for dancing. Wait, that was from my sixth grade wedding.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you couldn't get them anyway. Ian doesn't plan anymore and Derrick... (Off of Rachel and Monica's looks) And Derrick is a name I shouldn't know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sitting up from the couch) Hey Mon, do you have another pillow? (Holds up one.) Y'know, something a little snugglyer?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why are you napping over here instead of over at your place?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, the duck...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! The duck?! What the hell did the damn duck do now?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, well he did not get sick somewhere in there and it was immediately found and properly cleaned up!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now, do I get to look at this book or is it just for people who are actually involved in the wedding?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course you can look at it! Yeah, I want your opinion too!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here you go! What do you think about centerpieces?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Centerpieces!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! Roses or Lilies? (Holds up a picture of each.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Definitely roses. (Monica and Rachel exchange a look.) Well, I just think they're a little more weddingy. (Monica holds the Lily picture closer to him.) But Lilies are the clear choice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! It's like one mind.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sitting up again) Guys! Guys!! You gotta let me nap! Ugh, I'm gonna get cranky!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, there is a perfectly good couch across the hall!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes it is perfectly good, and it is not one of the places the duck got sick!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I'm gonna go! (Gets up and heads for the door.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now Joey, what did the duck do?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know! But he did not eat your face cream!
[Cut to Joey and Rachel's, Joey enters and heads for his bedroom. He pushes open the door to find the duck.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey little buddy, how are you feeling? (The duck does not get sick and Joey recoils in horror and heads for the couch.) What the hell is in that face cream? (He's about to try out the couch but notices the bed in Rachel's room. He walks into her room and feels the bed.) That's so soft. (He pulls back the comforter.) Pillowcases! (He climbs in and groans in delight. Suddenly, he feels something under him and pulls out a little beat up paperback book. He opens it and starts to read from it.) (In his head.) Zelda looked at the chimney sweep. Her father, the vicar...(Stops reading and thinks.) The vicar? (Continues reading) ...wouldn't be home for hours. Her loins were burning. She threw caution to the wind and reached out and grabbed his...(Out loud.) Whoa! (Reads on in silence.) Whoa-ho-ho-ho! This is a dirty book! (Continues to read.)
Ross's apartment, Phoebe has moved in and has a massage client on her table she's set up in the living room. Ross enters and is shocked to see a naked man lying on the table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Phoebe...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Ross, hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm with a client right now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, let's talk outside.
They go into the hall.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, you can't massage people in my apartment!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's the big deal? I did it at Monica and Chandler's!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And they knew about it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (pause as she considers it) Okay, well Ross, what is this really about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, this is my home and I want to be able to come and go whenever I want!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I will find someplace else to do the rest of my appointments. I just don't know what the big deal is!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The big deal is I don't want naked, greasy strangers in my apartment when I want to kick back with a puzzle-beer! Cold beer.
Joey and Rachel's, Joey is sweeping up as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Joey, what 'cha doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sweepin'. Why? Turn you on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh. What if I was sweeping a chimney?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, did you my face cream?
She walks into her bedroom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where are you going? The vicar won't be home for hours.
She comes back out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, (nervously) where did you learn that word?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where do you think, (pause) Zelda?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gasps) You found my book?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah I did!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, what-what are you doing going into my bedroom?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, look I'm sorry, I went in there to take a nap and I know I shouldn't have, but you got porn!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey-hey, y'know what? I don't care! I'm not ashamed of my book. There's nothing with a woman enjoying a little...erotica. It's just a healthy expression of female sexuality, which by the way, you will never understand. (She goes into her room.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got porn!

Ross's apartment, Ross is coming out of the living room carrying his salad and a puzzle-beer! Cold beer. And he decides to fold up Phoebe's massage table, but being Ross has trouble with it as there is a knock on the door. He sets the table back up and opens the door to reveal a beautiful woman.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hi, is uh Phoebe here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh no-no, she-she's out for the night.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Ohh great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can I, can I help you with something?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Well, I don't know. Are you a masseur?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (deadpan) Yes I am.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Great! (Calls down the hall) Dad! (Her old father walks in.) Thank you so much, I'll be back to pick him up in an hour. (She walks away.)
Ross isn't happy and closes the door slowly.
A Restaurant, Monica and Chandler are having dinner with her parents.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: So Chandler, you're parents must've been thrilled when you told them you were engaged.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, I should probably call them.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I remember when we first got engaged.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I don't think I ever heard that story.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh dad, really you don't need to...

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (ignoring her) Well, I'd gotten Judy pregnant. I still don't know that happened.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (incredulous) You don't know how that happened?! Your dog thought my diaphragm was a chew toy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What a sweet story.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, at least you're not hearing it for the first time at your fifth grade Halloween party.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: What?! They wanted a scary story!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Anyway, we're really excited about our wedding plans, and well I guess pretty soon we'll be making a big withdrawal from the Monica wedding fund. (Chandler and her laugh, but her parents don't.) What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: You tell her Jack, I can't do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What happened? You still have the Monica wedding fund don't you?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: We have it. Only now, we call it the beach house.
Commercial Break
A Restaurant, scene continued from before the break.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't believe you spent my wedding fund on the beach house!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: We're sorry honey, but we just assumed if you got married after you turned 30 you'd pay for it yourself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You bought the beach house when I was 23!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Which means you had seven years of beach fun and you can't put a price on that sweetie.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: We really do feel bad about this though.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: We started saving again when you were dating Richard and then that went to hell, so we redid the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about when I started dating Chandler?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Well it was Chandler! We didn't think he'd ever propose!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Clearly I did not start drinking enough at the start of the meal. (Starts to make up for lost time and takes a big swig of his drink.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe it! That there is no money for my wedding?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: We might still have some money, if your father didn't think it was a good idea to sell ice over the Internet.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: It seemed like such a simple idea.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Stupid Jack, the word is stupid.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: All right, enough! I don't want to hear about it anymore! (Under his breath) Good luck, Chandler. (Chandler takes another drink.)
Ross's apartment, his massage client is on the table and Ross is reluctantly starting his massage. He spreads some lotion in his hands, and doesn't like it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay! Now, I'm going to touch you. (He does so, very gingerly.) Ohh, that's soft. (He starts poking him and notices his salad spoons and starts to massage him with those.)
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica and Chandler are returning from dinner, Rachel is already there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe this. Do you think that your parents could help pay for it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know, my mother spent most of her money on her fourth wedding. She's saving the rest for her divorce. And any extra cash my father has he saves for his yearly trips to (Pause) Dollywood.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well what happened at dinner?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My parents spent the money for our wedding!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (gasps) My God! What did you order?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, but there's no money! Well this is terrible! You guys are gonna have to get married in like a, rec. center!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, it's gonna be okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! No it's not! It's not gonna be okay! It sucks! No swing band! No lilies!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, y'know what? It's gonna be okay. I mean you don't have to have this rustic Italian feast. Y'know? And-and you don't need, you don't need this custom-made, empire waisted, duchess, satin gown; you can wear off the rack. (She starts to cry, as does Monica.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, it really is gonna be okay. The important thing is that we love each other and that we're gonna get married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you even understand what off the rack means?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look, why don't you just pay for it yourself?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How? I don't have any money.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I have some.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, close to... (Notices Rachel leaning in to hear and decides to write it on a piece of paper and hand it to Monica as Phoebe averts her eyes.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Whoa!!! Are you kidding me?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well what?! How-how much is it?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's enough for wedding scenario eight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! (Whispers.) Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Chandler) How great are you, you little saver?! I mean, the-the amount you have is exactly the budget of my dream wedding!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starting to cry) Ohh, you guys are so made for each other.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you're not suggesting that we spend all of the money on the wedding?

lines.speaker: Rachel and Monica
lines.text: Ah, yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, come on, I've been saving this money for six years and I kinda had some of it earmarked for the future, not just for a party.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (reading the slip of paper) Wow! (In a sultry voice) Hello, Mr. Chandler.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is the most special day of our lives.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I realize that honey, but I'm not gonna spend all of the money on one party.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, umm I-I love you, (laughs) but umm, if you call our wedding a party one more time, you may not get invited. Okay? (Laughs) Listen, we could always earn more money, okay? But uh, we're only gonna get married once.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I understand, but I have to put my foot down. Okay? The answer is no.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You-you're gonna have to put your foot down?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, I am!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, money and a firm hand. Finally a Chandler I can get on board with.
Central Perk, Joey and Ross are there as Rachel enters and sees Joey sitting there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (quietly) Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach, do you smell smoke?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh, I get it, smoke, chimney, chimney sweep, very funny, ha-ha.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no, I'm serious. You don't smell it? Something's on fire.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well no, I don't smell anything.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, y'know what? It's probably just your burnin' loins.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sitting down) Hey, what are you guys, what are you guys talking about?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (takes a drink) Damn, this coffee's cold! Hey Rach, do you mind if I heat this up on your loins? (Joey and he both laugh.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, I can not believe you told him, Joey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So I guess you bought that book after we broke up huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh, yeah I did, because I wore out my first copy when I was with you. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (chases her) Oh yeah, yeah? Well uh, when we were going out, I read tons of porno magazines! (Realizes a table of women overheard him.) (To that table.) 'Sup?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Ross! How could you do that to an old man?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking at the table) Excuse me ladies. (To Phoebe) I'm sorry?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My massage client, Arthur? His daughter called and said that some guy that worked for me gave him a really weird massage this afternoon.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (incredulous) I gave him an extremely professional massage!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He said you poked at him with wooden spoons.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, so it wasn't uh, a traditional massage. But I did give him accu-pressure with a pair of chopsticks. And, and I gently exfoliated him with, with a mop.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he's never coming back! Okay? You just cost me eight dollars a week!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y'know what? This is your fault! You're the one that didn't move his-his appointment.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's my fault?! You didn't have to massage him! You could've sent him away! You could've not rolled Tonka trucks up and down his back!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He said he liked that!! Oh you're right, you're right. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, what are you massaging an old man for?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: His daughter was hot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Gotcha.
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler is looking at the wedding book as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen umm, I've been thinking, it's not fair for me to ask you to spend all of your money on our wedding. I mean, you work, you work really hard for that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ehh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Eh, you work for that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I thought about it too, and I'm sorry. I think we should spend all of the money on the wedding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You do?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I'm putting my foot down. Yeah look, when I proposed I told you that I would do anything to make you happy, and if having the perfect wedding makes you happy then, then that's what we're gonna do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you're so sweet. (They hug and kiss.) Oh, but wait, what about our, what about the future and stuff?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh, forget about the future and stuff! So we only have two kids, y'know? We'll pick our favorite and that one will get to go to college.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You thought about that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How many kids were we gonna have?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, four, a boy, twin girls and another boy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What else did you think about?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, stuff like where'd we live, y'know? Like a small place outside the city, where our kids could learn to ride their bikes and stuff. Y'know, we could have a cat that had a bell on it's collar and we could hear it every time it ran through the little kitty door. Of course, we'd have an apartment over the garage where Joey could grow old.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs) Y'know what? I-I don't want a big, fancy wedding.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure you do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I want everything you just said. I want a marriage.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You sure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-hmm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I love you so much.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love you. (They kiss.) Hey listen umm, when, when you were talkin' about our future you said cat, but you meant dog right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, totally!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good.
Ending Credits
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is balancing her checkbook as Joey enters from his room wearing a hockey helmet, gloves, and shin guards.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello, Zelda.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who are you supposed to be?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The vicar!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you even know what a vicar is?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Like a goalie, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sarcastically) Yeah. Look Joey, it's enough all right?! You keep making these stupid jokes and this sleazy innuendoes and it's-I'm not-it's just not funny anymore!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I'm sorry. Rach I-Rach I'm sorry. Okay? I'm sorry! Maybe I can make up for it by, taking you roughly in the barn. (Giggles.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right! Y'know what? That's it! You wanna do it?! Let's do it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starting to move closer to him) That's right, I wanna do it with you! I've been trying to fight it, but you just said all the right things.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (nervously backing away) I-I-I-I did? (He puts a stool in front of her.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (moves the stool out of the way) Yeah! Ohh, I've been waitin' so long to get on that body!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This body? (He backs into the kitchen.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah that's right! Come on Joey; sex me up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey, you're startin' to sound like the butcher's wife there in-in chapter seven.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on now, don't keep me waiting. Get those clothes off! But, I would keep that helmet on because you're in for a rough ride! (He backs into the door.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't want to, I'm scared.
Rachel walks away, pleased with her self.
End
